error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to TimescaleDB. Check the DSN, ensure the database is running, and verify the user has CREATE TABLE privileges.

data_description.write_batch_size_records: Number of state changes to buffer before writing to the database. Higher values reduce write frequency but increase memory use.
data_description.flush_interval_seconds: Maximum seconds between database writes, even if the batch is not full.
data_description.chunk_interval_days: Days per hypertable chunk. Only affects new chunks — existing chunks keep their original interval. Smaller chunks compress sooner and use less peak disk space.
data_description.compress_after_hours: Hours before a closed chunk is compressed. Compressed chunks are read-only. The compression policy runs at half this interval (max 12 h). Must be at least the chunk interval in hours.

buffer_dropping.title: TimescaleDB recorder buffer overflow
buffer_dropping.description: The TimescaleDB recorder in-memory buffer filled up and is dropping state events. This usually means the TimescaleDB database has been unavailable for an extended period. Dropped events will be recovered automatically from Home Assistant's sqlite recorder once the database is reachable again. No user action is required; this issue will clear itself.

states_worker_stalled.title: TimescaleDB recorder states worker stalled
states_worker_stalled.description: The TimescaleDB recorder states worker has failed more than 5 consecutive write attempts to the TimescaleDB database. The worker will continue retrying indefinitely with exponential backoff (capped at 60s). This issue will clear automatically on the next successful write. If it persists, check the HA log for tracebacks and verify that the database is reachable and the schema is compatible.

meta_worker_stalled.title: TimescaleDB recorder metadata worker stalled
meta_worker_stalled.description: The TimescaleDB recorder metadata worker has failed more than 5 consecutive write attempts. Registry changes (entities, devices, areas, labels) are queued on disk and will replay when the database is reachable again. This issue will clear automatically on the next successful write. If it persists, check the HA log for tracebacks.

db_unreachable.title: TimescaleDB database unreachable
db_unreachable.description: The TimescaleDB recorder has been unable to reach the database for more than 5 minutes. State changes and registry updates are buffered in memory (capped at 10,000 events) and on disk (metadata queue). Data is preserved and will be written once connectivity is restored. This issue will clear automatically on the next successful write.

recorder_disabled.title: Home Assistant recorder integration is disabled
recorder_disabled.description: The Home Assistant recorder integration is not loaded or has been disabled. The TimescaleDB recorder relies on HA's sqlite recorder as a safety net for backfilling state changes after a database outage. Without it, events dropped during a TimescaleDB outage cannot be recovered. To fix this, re-enable the recorder integration in your configuration and restart Home Assistant.
